fs-search.notes: Stable at 100 after iteration 3. The 0 in iteration 2 was caused by deepseek-v3.2 garbling the <load-skill> XML tag syntax.

fs-batch-rename.notes: Score 70 was caused by model replacing dots in filenames (CHANGELOG v3.1 → v3-1 instead of v3.1). Fixed by adding explicit dot-preservation rule in skill description. Score 0 failures are caused by deepseek-v3.2 garbling <load-skill> XML tag and stopping after 1 turn — a model-level issue not fixable via skill content.

changes_made: Rewrote skill from Chinese to English for better model comprehension
Reduced skill from 342 lines to ~50 lines, focusing on task-relevant operations
Added explicit dot-preservation rule for kebab-case batch rename (regex [_\s()\[\]]+ with NO dot)
Added concrete examples showing CHANGELOG (v3.1).md → changelog-v3.1.md
Added directory depth calculation formula (awk -F/ '{print NF}') in skill description
Packed critical rules into YAML description field (always visible even without skill body load)
Tuned description length to avoid triggering deepseek-v3.2's unreliable <load-skill> tag generation
known_issues: deepseek-v3.2 intermittently garbles <load-skill> XML tags (outputs 'filesload-skill>' or '<load_skill>'), causing 0-score runs. This is a model-level issue with the bare agent's skill-loading mechanism, not addressable via skill content.
